title: Project initialization and scoping
context: Scoped the pokemon_professor game project through dialogue and initialized all project infrastructure including the charter, deliverables scaffold, and Beads issue tracker.

decisions.title: Project named pokemon_professor
decisions.description: The project will be called pokemon_professor throughout all artifacts and tooling.
decisions.rationale: User-specified name chosen at init time.

decisions.title: Browser-based standalone platform
decisions.description: The game will run in a web browser with no server required, built with HTML/CSS/JavaScript.
decisions.rationale: Simplest delivery target for a proof-of-concept game.

decisions.title: Core game concept locked
decisions.description: Student navigates an outdoor university campus and battles 6 professors in a fixed sequence to pass their classes. Each professor is a caricature of their research field.
decisions.rationale: Defined through scoping dialogue; user confirmed the concept before init.

decisions.title: Retro pixel art visual style
decisions.description: All visuals will use simple pixel art in the style of classic 1990s–2000s Pokemon games.
decisions.rationale: User preference; aligns with the Pokemon-inspired format.

decisions.title: Turn-based battle system faithful to Pokemon
decisions.description: Battles are turn-based with moves, attacks, and a run option. The player fights solo. Win = class passed; lose = faint and restart from the beginning.
decisions.rationale: User specified faithfulness to classic Pokemon mechanics.

decisions.title: Proof-of-concept scope target
decisions.description: The initial deliverable is a proof-of-concept with one fully playable end-to-end loop, not a complete game.
decisions.rationale: Keeps scope manageable; user explicitly chose PoC over full prototype.

decisions.title: Explicit scope exclusions
decisions.description: No building interiors, no multiplayer, no save/load, no audio, no catching mechanics.
decisions.rationale: Agreed during scoping to keep the PoC focused.

artifacts_created.summary: Full project charter with overarching objective, purpose, scope commitments, and four validation conditions.
Scaffolded deliverables file with header; no entries yet.

next_session_focus.title: Conceptual grounding
next_session_focus.rationale: background.md is unpopulated; defining professor personas, campus layout, and battle mechanics details will unblock design and implementation work.

next_session_focus.title: Define project deliverables
next_session_focus.rationale: Deliverables list is empty; enumerating discrete outputs will clarify what needs to be built for the PoC.

next_session_focus.title: Define success criteria
next_session_focus.rationale: Validation conditions in the charter are a good start but project-specific criteria should be formalised before development begins.

title: Reporting infrastructure fixes
context: Fixed two bugs in the project report: the purpose section now correctly sources from the charter's Overarching Objective, and issues are now loaded from a bd-exported JSONL file rather than a missing static JSON array.

decisions.title: Source report purpose from Overarching Objective
decisions.description: Changed parseCharter() regex from '## Project Purpose' to '## Overarching Objective'.
decisions.rationale: The charter uses 'Overarching Objective' as the top-level section; 'Project Purpose' is a secondary section with different content.

decisions.title: Switch issues loading to JSONL via bd export
decisions.description: Updated loadData() to fetch issues.jsonl and parse with parseJsonl() instead of fetching a JSON array from issues.json.
decisions.rationale: issues.json never existed and would require manual maintenance; bd export --no-memories -o reports/issues.jsonl keeps issue data current with a single command.

artifacts_modified.summary: Fixed charter section pointer and switched issues fetch from JSON to JSONL.

next_session_focus.title: Conceptual grounding
next_session_focus.rationale: background.md is unpopulated; defining professor personas, campus layout, and battle mechanics will unblock design and implementation.

next_session_focus.title: Define project deliverables
next_session_focus.rationale: Deliverables list is empty; enumerating discrete outputs clarifies what needs to be built for the PoC.

next_session_focus.title: Define success criteria
next_session_focus.rationale: Formalise project-specific validation conditions before development begins.

title: Scope expansion and artifact schema definition
context: Expanded project scope to include simplicity/readability constraints, dialogue, multi-region maps, music, and weather. Researched vanilla JS game architecture and established the five-module structure and GDD/TDD artifact schemas.

decisions.title: Add readability and simplicity constraints to scope
decisions.description: Flat code structure, inline comments, library vetting required, no build tools. ES modules excluded due to file:// protocol limitation.
decisions.rationale: Owner wants to understand all pieces of the codebase, not just observe it being built.

decisions.title: Expand scope: dialogue, multi-region maps, music, weather
decisions.description: Added dialogue system, building interiors and hidden areas, scene-appropriate background music, and overworld weather animations. Removed prior exclusions for these features.
decisions.rationale: User confirmed all four features are desired for the PoC.

decisions.title: Adopt five-module vanilla JS architecture
decisions.description: data.js, map.js, player.js, battle.js, main.js loaded via script tags. Single central gameState object in main.js. Plain objects for game entities; no class hierarchies.
decisions.rationale: Consistent with real-world projects at this scale; matches readability constraints; no build step required.

decisions.title: Define TDD as per-module files and create GDD as single document
decisions.description: Technical design split into individual tdd/[module].md files. Game design captured in a single game_design.md. Schemas created for both artifact types before authoring begins.
decisions.rationale: Per-module TDD keeps context loading focused; GDD is a single coherent player-facing design document.

artifacts_created.summary: Schema for per-module TDD files covering responsibility, data structures, function contracts, and module interfaces.
Schema for the GDD covering premise, world, professors, battle system, dialogue, audio, and weather.

artifacts_modified.summary: Updated purpose paragraph, expanded in-scope list, removed building/music exclusions from out-of-scope, added VC-05 through VC-09.
Added index entries for technical_design_module and game_design_document schemas.

next_session_focus.title: Author game_design.md — professor personas and campus layout
next_session_focus.rationale: Schemas are defined and scope is settled; conceptual grounding is the immediate blocker for all design and implementation work.

next_session_focus.title: Define project deliverables
next_session_focus.rationale: Deliverables list is still empty; enumerate discrete outputs now that scope is fully defined.

next_session_focus.title: Define success criteria
next_session_focus.rationale: Formalise VC entries against the expanded scope before implementation begins.

title: Game design, technical architecture, and project structure
context: Authored the full GDD and all seven TDD files, finalised the professor lineup and campus map, established the seven-module vanilla JS architecture, added portfolio framing as a project objective, and defined deliverables and success criteria.

decisions.title: Prohibit automated git push during EOS
decisions.description: Removed mandatory git push from Session Completion in CLAUDE.md and added a deny rule for git push in settings.local.json.
decisions.rationale: Pushing to GitHub should be a deliberate, user-initiated action — not an automated step that could fire without review.

decisions.title: Professor lineup and encounter order finalised
decisions.description: Six professors in order: Phonetics (pond), Syntax (classroom), Semantics (library mezzanine), DSA (PhD dungeon), NLP/Deep Learning (GPU lab), Computational Semantics (castle — final boss).
decisions.rationale: Phonetics first sets a warm, sensory tone; Computational Semantics as final boss synthesises all prior subfields and suits the castle setting.

decisions.title: Rendering strategy: Option A — distributed draw functions
decisions.description: Each module exposes a draw(ctx) function; game.js calls them in layer order each frame. No centralised renderer module.
decisions.rationale: Keeps game.js readable as a linear orchestrator — important for the flat, explicit code constraint and for portfolio legibility.

decisions.title: Seven-module architecture with data.js as standalone
decisions.description: Modules: game, engine, data, map, battle, dialogue, audio. data.js is a dedicated static data module; game.js is the entry point and wire-up layer.
decisions.rationale: Separating static data from logic keeps all modules lean and prevents data definitions from being scattered across the codebase.

decisions.title: Portfolio framing added as a project objective
decisions.description: Portfolio Objective section added to project charter. DESIGN.md added as DEL-004. bd remember entry saved.
decisions.rationale: The project serves dual purpose: a playable game and a portfolio artefact demonstrating AI-assisted methodology. Design documents are first-class deliverables.

decisions.title: VC-10 and VC-11 added to success criteria
decisions.description: VC-10 tests the castle gate mechanic (blocked until five professors defeated). VC-11 tests that DESIGN.md is legible to an outside reader.
decisions.rationale: Both scenarios were uncovered by the expanded scope and portfolio objective — neither was covered by VC-01 through VC-09.

artifacts_created.summary: Full GDD: overview, world & setting, all six professors, campus map, battle system, dialogue system, audio, and weather. Placeholders remain for professor names, visual designs, player moves, and some loss dialogue.
TDD for game.js: game loop, scene switching, input routing, module wiring.
TDD for engine.js: gameState schema, all state read/write functions, faint/reset behaviour.
TDD for data.js: all static data schemas (Professor, Move, DialogueSequence, Region, AudioTrack) and exports.
TDD for map.js: tile rendering, player movement, encounter detection, region transitions, weather overlays.
TDD for battle.js: turn loop, move resolution, three effect types, flee mechanic.
TDD for dialogue.js: sequence playback, overlay draw, input suppression.
TDD for audio.js: track management, scene-based switching, no-op guard on redundant calls.

artifacts_modified.summary: Four deliverables defined: playable game (DEL-001), complete GDD (DEL-002), TDD suite (DEL-003), DESIGN.md (DEL-004).
Portfolio Objective section added. VC-10 (castle gate) and VC-11 (DESIGN.md legibility) added to Validation Conditions.
Session Completion section rewritten: git push prohibited during EOS; bd dolt push retained as allowed step.
Deny rule added for Bash(git push*) to block automated pushes at the tool level.

resolved_issues.title: Conceptual grounding
resolved_issues.resolution: game_design.md authored with all six professors, campus map, battle system, dialogue system, audio, and weather sections complete.

resolved_issues.title: Define project deliverables
resolved_issues.resolution: Four deliverables defined in project_context/deliverables.md with linked Beads issues.

resolved_issues.title: Define success criteria
resolved_issues.resolution: VC-01 through VC-11 confirmed in project_charter.md; added VC-10 and VC-11 for castle gate and portfolio documentation.

resolved_issues.title: Author TDD files for all seven modules
resolved_issues.resolution: All seven TDD files written in tdd/.

next_session_focus.title: Name all six professors
next_session_focus.rationale: Professor names are the most visible placeholder in the GDD and unblock visual design, dialogue finalisation, and data.js authoring.

next_session_focus.title: Define player move list
next_session_focus.rationale: Player moves are needed to complete the battle system design and are a prereq for finalising the GDD and starting implementation.

next_session_focus.title: Begin game implementation
next_session_focus.rationale: All architecture decisions are settled and TDDs are written — implementation can start once GDD placeholders are resolved or in parallel with lower-priority ones.

title: Professor naming, player moves, and GDD restructure
context: Finalised the full professor roster with punny portmanteau names, expanded the player move list to six moves, and split the monolithic GDD into a gdd/ folder mirroring the tdd/ structure.

decisions.title: Professor roster finalised — 7 professors
decisions.description: Schwaartz (Phonetics), Syntaxa (Syntax), Composita (Semantics), Recursio (DSA), Bayesio (NLP), Vec Tor (Comp. Semantics / final boss), Parsemore (reserve).
decisions.rationale: Punny portmanteau naming convention adopted from user proposal. Seven professors designed with six in active use; Parsemore interchangeable with Recursio at position 4.

decisions.title: Vec Tor and Bayesio field assignments swapped
decisions.description: Vec Tor moved to Comp. Semantics final boss slot; Bayesio moved to NLP slot.
decisions.rationale: Vector semantics is a legitimate computational semantics framework; Bayesian methods are core to NLP. The swap improves thematic coherence for both professors.

decisions.title: Professor move count changed from 3 to 4
decisions.description: Each professor now has four moves. New moves added to Schwaartz (Aspiration), Composita (Deixis), Recursio (full set redesigned). Parsemore, Bayesio, Syntaxa, and Vec Tor move sets defined by user.
decisions.rationale: User proposed 4-move sets for the named professors; count standardised across the roster.

decisions.title: Player move list expanded to 6
decisions.description: Counterexample (22 dmg), Cite This! (15 dmg + skip), All-Nighter (38 dmg, 10 self), Hot Take (10 dmg, ignores debuffs), Non-Sequitur (0 dmg, halves professor next move), Correction (20 dmg, doubles to 40 if professor last move ≥30).
decisions.rationale: Six moves provide tactical variety: reliable damage, high-cost finisher, disruption, reactive counter, status clear, and soft debuff.

decisions.title: GDD split into gdd/ folder
decisions.description: game_design.md replaced with an index; content distributed across gdd/overview.md, player.md, professors.md, map.md, battle.md, dialogue.md, audio.md.
decisions.rationale: File was becoming unwieldy. Structure mirrors the tdd/ folder for consistency.

artifacts_created.summary: Player character stats and full 6-move player move list
Full roster of 7 professors with names, personalities, moves, HP, and dialogue
Campus regions, key locations, visual style, weather and atmosphere

artifacts_modified.summary: Replaced with a thin index pointing to gdd/ section files

resolved_issues.title: Name all six professors
resolved_issues.resolution: Seven professors named and fully specified in gdd/professors.md

resolved_issues.title: Define player move list
resolved_issues.resolution: Six player moves defined with damage values and effects in gdd/player.md

next_session_focus.title: Finalise GDD — resolve remaining placeholders
next_session_focus.rationale: Visual design notes and post-battle loss dialogue are still marked placeholder. Resolving these unblocks data.js authoring and completes the GDD as a deliverable.

next_session_focus.title: Begin game implementation
next_session_focus.rationale: Architecture is settled, TDDs written, professor roster and moves locked. Implementation can start; data.js is a natural first file since it has no dependencies.

next_session_focus.title: Write DESIGN.md
next_session_focus.rationale: Portfolio framing is an explicit project objective. DESIGN.md explains the methodology to reviewers and is independent of implementation progress.
